problems.title: Is Leap Year
problems.problem_text: Given a year y as a positive integer, return True iff y is a Gregorian leap year (divisible by 4, except century years which must also be divisible by 400).
problems.reference_solution:
def is_leap_year(y):
    return y % 4 == 0 and (y % 100 != 0 or y % 400 == 0)

    return y % 4 == 0

variants.name: returns-true
variants.code:
def is_leap_year(y):
    return True

variants.name: missed-400-clause
variants.code:
def is_leap_year(y):
    return y % 4 == 0 and y % 100 != 0

problems.title: Minimum Coin Change
problems.problem_text: Given a list of positive integer coin denominations and a non-negative target amount, return the minimum number of coins needed to make the amount, or -1 if impossible. You may use each coin denomination unlimited times.

variants.name: returns-amount
variants.code:
def min_coins(coins, amount):
    return amount

variants.name: greedy-largest-first
variants.code:
def min_coins(coins, amount):
    coins = sorted(coins, reverse=True)
    n = 0
    for c in coins:
        n += amount // c
        amount = amount % c
    return n if amount == 0 else -1

variants.name: no-impossible-handling

problems.title: Summary Ranges
problems.problem_text: Given a sorted list of distinct integers nums, return a list of strings describing each maximal contiguous range. Single-element ranges are 'n'; multi-element ranges are 'lo->hi'. Example: [0,1,2,4,5,7] returns ['0->2', '4->5', '7'].
problems.reference_solution:
def summary_ranges(nums):
    if not nums: return []
    out = []
    start = nums[0]
    prev = nums[0]
    for n in nums[1:]:
        if n == prev + 1:
            prev = n
        else:
            out.append(str(start) if start == prev else f'{start}->{prev}')
            start = n
            prev = n
    out.append(str(start) if start == prev else f'{start}->{prev}')
    return out

test_cases.description: mix of ranges and singles with negatives

variants.name: single-elem-each
variants.code:
def summary_ranges(nums):
    return [str(n) for n in nums]

variants.name: first-and-last-only
returns-empty
variants.code:
def summary_ranges(nums):
    return []

problems.title: Reverse Vowels
problems.problem_text: Given a string s, return s with only the vowels reversed (in their positions). Vowels are 'a','e','i','o','u' (case-insensitive). Non-vowel characters keep their positions.

variants.name: reverses-whole-string
variants.code:
def reverse_vowels(s):
    return s[::-1]

variants.name: returns-input
variants.code:
def reverse_vowels(s):
    return s

variants.name: case-sensitive-vowels
variants.code:
def reverse_vowels(s):
    chars = list(s)
    vowels = set('aeiou')
    i, j = 0, len(chars) - 1
    while i < j:
        while i < j and chars[i] not in vowels: i += 1
        while i < j and chars[j] not in vowels: j -= 1
        chars[i], chars[j] = chars[j], chars[i]
        i += 1
        j -= 1
    return ''.join(chars)

problems.title: Add Strings
problems.problem_text: Given two non-negative integers represented as strings num1 and num2, return their sum as a string. You must not convert the inputs to integers directly.
problems.reference_solution:
def add_strings(num1, num2):
    i, j = len(num1) - 1, len(num2) - 1
    carry = 0
    out = []
    while i >= 0 or j >= 0 or carry:
        a = int(num1[i]) if i >= 0 else 0
        b = int(num2[j]) if j >= 0 else 0
        s = a + b + carry
        out.append(str(s % 10))
        carry = s // 10
        i -= 1
        j -= 1
    return ''.join(reversed(out))

variants.name: no-carry
variants.code:
def add_strings(num1, num2):
    a = num1.zfill(max(len(num1), len(num2)))
    b = num2.zfill(max(len(num1), len(num2)))
    out = ''
    for x, y in zip(a, b):
        out += str(int(x) + int(y))
    return out

variants.name: concatenates
variants.code:
def add_strings(num1, num2):
    return num1 + num2

variants.name: returns-first
variants.code:
def add_strings(num1, num2):
    return num1

problems.title: Softmax (1D, Numerically Stable)
problems.problem_text: Given a list of real numbers xs (non-empty), return a list of the same length whose elements are softmax(xs) = exp(xs - max(xs)) / sum(exp(xs - max(xs))). Each output value rounded to 4 decimal places. Subtract max before exponentiating to avoid overflow.
problems.reference_solution:
def softmax(xs):
    import math
    m = max(xs)
    exps = [math.exp(x - m) for x in xs]
    s = sum(exps)
    return [round(e / s, 4) for e in exps]

variants.name: no-shift-overflows
variants.code:
def softmax(xs):
    import math
    exps = [math.exp(x) for x in xs]
    s = sum(exps)
    return [round(e / s, 4) for e in exps]

variants.name: no-normalize
variants.code:
def softmax(xs):
    import math
    m = max(xs)
    return [round(math.exp(x - m), 4) for x in xs]

variants.name: returns-input
variants.code:
def softmax(xs):
    return [round(x, 4) for x in xs]

problems.title: Linear Regression (1D, Least Squares)
problems.problem_text: Given two lists xs and ys (same length, len >= 2) of floats, fit y = slope*x + intercept by least squares and return (slope, intercept) as a tuple, each rounded to 4 decimals.
problems.reference_solution:
def linear_regression(xs, ys):
    n = len(xs)
    mx = sum(xs) / n
    my = sum(ys) / n
    num = sum((x - mx) * (y - my) for x, y in zip(xs, ys))
    den = sum((x - mx) ** 2 for x in xs)
    slope = num / den
    intercept = my - slope * mx
    return (round(slope, 4), round(intercept, 4))

variants.name: no-mean-correction
variants.code:
def linear_regression(xs, ys):
    n = len(xs)
    num = sum(x * y for x, y in zip(xs, ys))
    den = sum(x * x for x in xs)
    slope = num / den
    intercept = sum(ys) / n - slope * sum(xs) / n
    return (round(slope, 4), round(intercept, 4))

variants.name: swapped-tuple
variants.code:
def linear_regression(xs, ys):
    n = len(xs)
    mx = sum(xs) / n
    my = sum(ys) / n
    num = sum((x - mx) * (y - my) for x, y in zip(xs, ys))
    den = sum((x - mx) ** 2 for x in xs)
    slope = num / den
    intercept = my - slope * mx
    return (round(intercept, 4), round(slope, 4))

variants.name: returns-zero-zero
variants.code:
def linear_regression(xs, ys):
    return (0.0, 0.0)

problems.title: Cumulative Distinct Count
problems.problem_text: Given a list of integers nums, return a list out where out[i] is the number of distinct values in nums[:i+1]. For [1,2,1,3] return [1,2,2,3].
problems.reference_solution:
def cumulative_distinct(nums):
    seen = set()
    out = []
    for n in nums:
        seen.add(n)
        out.append(len(seen))
    return out
